what: Semantic model was renamed to OEMInsightBI_v2 (spec corrected at lines 82/726; live report binds to ../OEMInsightBI_v2.SemanticModel; old model archived under fabric/archive/), but the archived name semantic_model_oeminsightbi still appears as the active/current model across ~10 non-archive, non-audit surfaces: README.md, project_definition.md, docs/README.md, docs/portfolio/PORTFOLIO_ASSETS_README.md, docs/architecture/fabric-artifacts-inventory.md (listed 'Active'), task files 002/003/004/009/013/014/015, commands sync-from-fabric/sync-to-fabric/run-full-pipeline, and support/documents (dax_measures.md, architecture/semantic_model.md, fabric_workspace.md, standards/naming_standards.md). Flagged by the 2026-05-17 coherence audit (path-drift lens) but the cross-surface sweep was never completed. NOTE: some occurrences are legitimate historical references that must NOT be changed (the 2026-05-17 audit files, feedback/archive.md, and the spec's 'old name archived' notes) -- distinguishing requires per-file judgment. Candidate for an /audit-coherence sweep; out of scope for task-010 (whose only doc occurrence, line 149, was already fixed).
resolution: Cross-surface sweep completed via task-021 (subtasks 1a-1d). Global grep confirms semantic_model_oeminsightbi no longer appears as an active reference outside fabric/archive/, designated naming examples, and task-file provenance. Also completed the sibling sweeps for clean_columnsAndHeaders.Notebook (re-opened FB-001) and report.Report. Regression guard added: tests/test_doc_artifact_names.py.

what: docs/portfolio/PORTFOLIO_ASSETS_README.md L55/L287 reference _Measures.tmdl, which exists only under fabric/archive/semantic_model_oeminsightbi.SemanticModel/. The canonical OEMInsightBI_v2 model reorganized measures into per-table .tmdl files, so _Measures.tmdl has no v2 equivalent. task-021_1a corrected the directory stem (rename sweep) but the leaf filename remains a broken reference -- separate content-drift, out of scope for the rename. Fix: point the portfolio README at the v2 model's actual measure file(s) or describe measures generically.
resolution: Fixed, and the finding was LARGER than recorded. The friction said the _Measures.tmdl leaf was a broken reference. Measured: the cited path combines the v2 DIRECTORY with the v1 FILENAME and exists nowhere, and all 8 'implemented' showcase measures live in the ARCHIVED v1 model (fabric/archive/semantic_model_oeminsightbi.SemanticModel, 46 measures). The canonical v2 model has 40 measures across 6 per-table files and shares only 'Total Spend EUR' and 'Avg EPI Score' with that list — the analytical measures (HHI Index, Spend-Weighted EPI Score, YoY Spend Growth %, Max Supply Concentration %, High Risk Material Count, % Spend - High EPI) were not carried into v2. A portfolio-facing doc was therefore asserting '✅ Complete' over measures the canonical model does not contain. All three references corrected, both models now named with their real paths and counts, and the v1->v2 trade flagged as something to narrate rather than discover. NOTE: whether to PORT the analytical measures into v2 is a decision left to Erik, not made here.

what: error_handling_strategy.md (L234/310/347/424/1071) and incremental_load_strategy.md (L540/543) use 'clean_columnsAndHeaders' as a pipeline ACTIVITY / config identifier. Ground-truth from fabric/orchestrator_pipeline_bronze_to_gold.DataPipeline/pipeline-content.json: the real cleaning activity is named 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' and references notebooks by notebookId (GUID), not by name -- there is NO 'clean_columnsAndHeaders' activity. So these design-doc identifiers are stale illustrative examples that never matched the real pipeline activity. task-021_1d PRESERVED them (per the task's preserve-activity-name instruction, whose premise turned out to be wrong) rather than guessing the correct identifier. Decision needed: rename to 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' (display name has spaces -> awkward in code examples), pick a code-identifier form, or leave as generic illustrative examples. Out of scope for the rename sweep.
resolution: Resolved 2026-07-20 by user decision: RENAME to the real artifact names. Applied context-sensitively across 9 occurrences in 3 files (FR-003 named only 2 — naming_standards.md was discovered during the fix). ACTIVITY contexts -> 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' (error_handling_strategy.md x5: retry table, pipeline JSON example, activity_name field comment, log call, log record example; incremental_load_strategy.md x1). NOTEBOOK context -> 'bronze-to-silver' (incremental_load_strategy.md:543). naming_standards.md x2 handled DIFFERENTLY: it cited clean_columnsAndHeaders as an EXAMPLE OF BAD NAMING, so a straight rename would have destroyed the point — the stale examples were replaced with real current artifacts illustrating the same inconsistency (snake_case data_quality_checks vs kebab-case silver-to-gold2). ALSO FIXED in passing (same code blocks, same class of error): activity type 'ExecuteNotebook' -> 'TridentNotebook' x4 — the real pipeline uses TridentNotebook; leaving a wrong type beside a corrected name would be half a fix. Verified: zero clean_columnsAndHeaders occurrences remain repo-wide.

what: Audit C-02 (spec names the EU supply-shares bronze table 'bronze_GlobalSupplyShares' in Data Architecture/Transformations L320/356/402 but 'bronze_EUSupplyShares' in Orchestration L626) was grounded during /iterate and found to mirror a REAL implementation split, not a spec typo. The pipeline copy activity 'bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares' writes sink table 'bronze_EUSupplyShares' (pipeline-content.json:42), but bronze-to-silver.Notebook (L135) and data_quality_checks.Notebook (4x) READ 'bronze_GlobalSupplyShares'. A whole-repo grep found NO writer/creator for 'bronze_GlobalSupplyShares' -- only the pipeline writing 'bronze_EUSupplyShares'. The semantic model PBI_RemovedChildren annotation lists BOTH names (a rename happened, one side not updated). LIKELY LINEAGE GAP: the orchestrated pipeline may not refresh the table silver consumes. PENDING: Erik to check oem_lh -- is bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (a) a manual rename of bronze_EUSupplyShares [then fix pipeline sink via /work], (b) a distinct intentional dataset [document both in spec], or (c) loaded externally [document load path, flag sink legacy]. Once confirmed: code fix via /work if needed, then reconcile spec via /iterate (standardize name or document both). C-03 + C-04 were resolved (spec edited 2026-06-22); C-02 deferred pending Fabric verification.
diagnostic_2026_06_22: Fabric check (Erik): BOTH tables exist in oem_lh, identical schema [Material,Stage,Country,Share,t], but FULLY DISJOINT data and different sizes. bronze_EUSupplyShares = 907 rows, lastModified 2026-01-19 (pipeline sink; read by nothing -> ORPHAN). bronze_GlobalSupplyShares = 56342 rows, lastModified 2025-08-25 (read by bronze-to-silver + DQ checks -> the actual silver/gold input, but STALE ~10mo and NOT refreshed by the pipeline). => distinct datasets + crossed lineage: fact_supply_share gold is built on Aug-2025 data; the pipeline's bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares produces an orphan. PENDING design decision from Erik: is bronze_GlobalSupplyShares meant to be pipeline-refreshed (=> bug, repoint copy activity to Global + retire EU) or an intentional static/external dataset (=> document + retire EU orphan)? Then /work fix + /iterate spec doc.
diagnostic_2_2026_06_22: Confirmed via the source GitHub repo (Erik's, public). The pipeline's HTTP copy activity (connection GUID 97124587-4fe9-43ee-bd6c-6a0fe3bc5664, desc 'Copy EU CRM github repo csv file') wires ONLY fact_EUSupplyShares&t.csv -> bronze_EUSupplyShares (907 rows = CSV 907, MATCHES). The broader fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv (2561 rows, the table the project actually wants since the project scope is global) is NOT wired into the pipeline. CRITICAL: bronze_GlobalSupplyShares = 56342 rows = EXACTLY 22x the 2561-row source CSV -> strongly suggests a duplicated/cross-joined load (or a different source); the bronze table does not match its documented source and feeds silver -> fact_supply_share. RESOLUTION DIRECTION (Erik clarified project is broader than the EU-focused report): wire a copy activity for fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv -> bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (mirror the EU copy activity) so the pipeline produces the correct 2561-row global table; retire/keep EU as secondary. PENDING: Erik to (1) confirm the 22x duplication via distinct-count query, (2) then a /work task adds the Global copy activity + verify gold numbers, (3) /iterate spec note.

what: incremental_load_strategy.md prescribes natural-key MERGE + dedupe (silver, L223-266) and MERGE + optional aggregation (gold, L277-344); task-024 now uses transaction-grain date-partition delete-insert on BOTH layers per Erik's binding 2026-07-14 decision (keep transaction grain). The strategy doc must be reconciled to describe delete-insert + the task-029 watermark dependency. Covered by task-033 (documentation drift sweep).
source_anchor: incremental_load_strategy.md § Silver Layer Strategy (223-266) and § Gold Layer Strategy (277-344)
resolution: Spec half reconciled by /iterate 2026-07-20 (friction pass). Spec Open Questions #1 now describes delete-insert as BUILT and the bronze_load_metadata watermark as PLANNED (task-029); usp_merge_fact_procurement reframed as a separate T-SQL demonstration. The support-doc half (incremental_load_strategy.md) remains task-033's scope.

what: DQ framework's silver<->gold spend reconciliation (Check 8) reconciles EXACTLY only under uniform base units: gold computes spend_eur = quantity_base * unitprice_eur where quantity_base is kg-normalized (silver-to-gold2 L1216-1221) but unitprice_eur is NOT converted, so silver SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur) != gold SUM(spend_eur) for any non-kg unit. task-026 therefore classified reconciliation ADVISORY (blocking it would false-fail); the fan-out it used to guard is instead caught BLOCKING by the new lookup/grain uniqueness checks. Root cause (unit normalization / unmapped units -> kg) is task-030's scope; once units are confirmed all-kg or unitprice is converted, reconciliation can be promoted to blocking.

WGI bronze table name drift: docs (schemas/bronze_tables.md, architecture/medallion_architecture.md, architecture/orchestration.md, architecture/data_sources.md, fabric_workspace.md) document 'bronze_WB_ESGCSV' + 'bronze_WB_ESGSeries', but NO live artifact produces them -- WGI_file2table.Dataflow produces a single table 'bronze_WGI' (Excel-sourced; columns Country Name, Country Code, Series Name, Percentile Rank 2023) which bronze-to-silver consumes and the semantic model references. The stale doc name MISLED task-026's WGI checks (caused a verify FAIL). Reconcile the docs to 'bronze_WGI'.
resolution: Spec half reconciled by /iterate 2026-07-20 (friction pass). Spec Data Transformations #3 replaced with the live bronze_WGI -> silver_wgi lineage; bronze/silver table inventories corrected; gold_dim_country and gold_dim_indicator WB sources corrected (dim_country is EPI-only; the WB indicator branch builds an empty DataFrame). The support-doc half remains task-033's scope. RESIDUALS NOT SWEPT (outside the approved change set, still require /iterate): spec_v1.md:818 is a CHECKED Current-State box '[x] WGI file ingestion (WGI_file2table.Dataflow)' asserting the retired dataflow lineage is done — the most misleading of the residuals; spec_v1.md:1580 Appendix header '**From silver_WB:**' describes sample patterns from a table that no longer exists. spec_v1.md:979 lists WGI_file2table as a naming-convention example, which is arguably legitimate as an example. Lines 248 and 374 intentionally NAME the retired lineage in supersession notes — do not 'fix' those.

what: pipeline-content.json now has 5 Stage-1 bronze activities (bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares added by task-022), but spec_v1.md § Orchestration L613-645 still enumerates 4, omits the Global copy activity, and L650/L710 assert 'All 4 bronze activities' / 'Stage 2 waits for all Stage 1 activities'. Same section also documents a Stage-4 'Copy job1' InvokeCopyJob that is absent from the pipeline (actual terminal activity: data_quality_checks TridentNotebook) and 'Retry: 0 attempts' where all activities carry retry:3. Reconciliation is acceptance #6 of task-022 and must route through /iterate.
resolution: Spec half reconciled by /iterate 2026-07-20 (friction pass). Spec Orchestration rewritten from pipeline-content.json ground truth: 5 Stage-1 activities (Global copy added), per-activity retry 1-3 (not 0), Stage 4 corrected from the nonexistent 'Copy job1' warehouse sync to data_quality_checks, warehouse sync marked planned-not-orchestrated, Stage-2 output silver_WB -> silver_wgi. Closes task-022 acceptance #6 on the spec side.

what: spec_v1.md § Data Architecture #4 (L289) lists silver_eusupplyshares in the current-state Bronze/Silver lineage table, but it has zero references across fabric/ and src/ (positive control: silver_globalsupplyshares returns 10 hits). bronze-to-silver.Notebook reads only bronze_GlobalSupplyShares, so the EU table remains orphaned at the silver boundary. Wiring EU into silver is DEC-001 Option B / task-038 work; the spec table should mark it planned rather than built. Introduced by the /iterate DEC-001 pass on 2026-07-20.
resolution: Spec half reconciled by /iterate 2026-07-20 (friction pass). Spec Data Architecture #4 now marks silver_eusupplyshares as planned (task-038) with an explicit orphaned-at-silver caveat. Root cause was the DEC-001 /iterate pass listing it as current-state lineage.

what: The spec's Testing Strategy section still states '33 tests for transformation logic in tests/ (task-008)', but the suite was already at 70 before this task and is now 87.

The root convention 'Testable transforms: Python modules in src/ with pytest coverage' reads as though the pipeline runs the tested code, but the notebooks duplicate the logic inline and import nothing from src/ — now mitigated by the documented reference-implementation contract plus parity tests, though the convention line still overstates coverage.
resolution: Root CLAUDE.md § Conventions corrected. The 'Testable transforms' line now states explicitly that src/ is a tested MIRROR, not the code the pipeline runs, and names the reference-implementation contract task-032 established: tests/ loads the notebook's own functions via load_notebook_functions and pins parity, with deliberate semantic gaps asserted rather than fixed. The convention no longer implies the pipeline executes the tested code.

what: The framework doc's severity table says a 0-49 quality score blocks the pipeline, but the implemented gate ignores score entirely and halts on status=='fail' over a fixed blocking set, so the doc teaches the exact misreading that caused this task.
resolution: The clarifying subsection distinguishing breach_flag from the gate already existed (task-040) but sat BELOW a table whose own 'Block pipeline' cell contradicted it. Corrected the table itself: header renamed to 'Intended Action', the 0-49 row no longer claims to block, and a warning above it marks the whole table aspirational. Also recorded that none of its notification actions are wired (every activity carries NoNotification) and that the only live mechanism is the advisory <70.0 breach_flag.

what: The spec's Data Quality section describes results being persisted to gold_quality_history but never mentions the blocking gate or its verdict; the gate appears only once, incidentally, in the orchestration section at line 650.

error_recovery_playbook.md documents a stale Fabric UI path for pipeline failure email ('Click Settings (gear icon) > Notifications'). The current surface is Home > Schedule > Failure notifications, and it carries a constraint the playbook does not mention: notifications are not sent for on-demand runs. The playbook also asserts 'They cannot be set via the pipeline JSON alone', true for pipeline-level email but overlooking that the three RefreshDataflow activities already carry a JSON-settable notifyOption that could be MailOnFailure.
resolution: Fabric UI path corrected to Home > Schedule > Failure notifications. Added the constraint the playbook omitted — notifications are schedule-attached and do NOT fire for on-demand runs, which is how this pipeline is run today (task-010 On Hold), so the setup is not coverage until scheduling lands. Also corrected the 'cannot be set via the pipeline JSON alone' claim: VERIFIED against pipeline-content.json that all three RefreshDataflow activities carry notifyOption='NoNotification', which is JSON-settable to MailOnFailure. Routed the choice to task-041 criterion 5 rather than configuring ad hoc.

what: task-041 criterion 1 asserts 'Fabric ANDs multiple dependsOn entries on one activity, so guarding N activities is not simply one handler with N dependencies'. The AND premise is correct and now Fabric-doc-confirmed, but the inference is wrong for this pipeline: the DAG is singly-terminal, so the documented 'Generic error handling' fan-in (one handler on the last activity via both Failed and Skipped) guards all 8 with one handler. The criterion's cost framing (1 vs 8) should be updated when the decision lands.
resolution: task-041 criterion 1 rewritten in place now that DEC-004 has landed. It records the settled topology (Option A, fan-in on the terminal activity + trailing Fail = 2 activities, not 8), the queryactivityruns mechanism, and the singly-terminal-DAG condition under which the choice must be revisited.

what: The /view-unmapped runbook already queries the restructured audit shape but calls the column unmapped_field with values supplier_hq_country/production_country/material, while task-027's acceptance criterion mandates unmapped_type with values material/hq_country/prod_country — the runbook now names a column that does not exist.
source_anchor: .claude/commands/view-unmapped.md § "Unmapped Values by Field" (the three spark.sql blocks filtering on unmapped_field)
resolution: Runbook realigned to task-027's shipped shape: unmapped_field -> unmapped_type across all 8 column references, and the value domain corrected from supplier_hq_country/production_country to hq_country/prod_country (verified against the unmapped_gap() call sites in silver-to-gold2). The runbook no longer queries a column that does not exist.

what: data_quality_architecture.md describes gold_gap_registry.total_occurrences as a cumulative count, but the audit tables it is fed from are full snapshots, so the implementation now stores the current-run occurrence count instead.
resolution: Reconciled in place by the /work orchestrator after task-027 verification. data_quality_architecture.md line ~302 now states current-run semantics with the reasoning, and line ~310's 'increment total_occurrences' was corrected to 'set' plus a new Reopened-gaps bullet. Support docs are directly editable under DEC-016. This also closes criterion 4's second clause, which verify-agent flagged as literally unclosed.

what: data_quality_architecture.md § [2] marks unmapped_value/unmapped_type/frequency/spend_impact as existing on gold_unmapped_procurement_audit; after this task unmapped_value, unmapped_type and spend_eur are real, but frequency remains an aggregate rather than a stored column and the four lifecycle fields live in gold_gap_registry by design.
resolution: Schema tree corrected against the shipped write_tbl call: actual columns are row_id, unmapped_type, gap_dimension, unmapped_value, spend_eur, detected_timestamp. Removed the two false entries — 'frequency' was never a stored column (the table is at occurrence grain; frequency is an aggregate, which is what /view-unmapped computes) and the spend column is spend_eur not spend_impact. The four lifecycle fields are no longer marked MISSING: they live on gold_gap_registry by design, because this table is overwritten every run and has no stable row to attach resolution state to. Status changed from PARTIAL to IMPLEMENTED.

what: task-028's EVIDENCE (A) listed three dead grp_map keys, but task-023 had already fixed all three the day before. The surviving half of the defect was in the alias table (3 of 7 alias LHS values had initcap(lhs) != lhs and were provably dead), which the evidence did not name. Task evidence written before a sibling task lands can send the implementer at the wrong half of a defect.
resolution: CLOSED AS A PROCESS LESSON, NOT AN ARTIFACT FIX. This entry records that task-028's EVIDENCE (A) pointed at three grp_map keys task-023 had fixed the day before, sending the implementer at the wrong half of the defect. There is no live artifact to correct — task-028 shipped and its evidence is now history. Misfiled as spec_implementation_gap (audit-eligible); the lesson is a workflow_deviation, which per rules/agents.md belongs in .session-log.jsonl only. Mirrored there; closing here so /audit-coherence stops clustering it as live drift.

what: gold_country_coverage_matrix's EPI probe left-joined on country_key then tested country_key IS NOT NULL — but country_key comes from the LEFT side of that join and is never null, so has_epi was hardcoded 1 and EVERY country was labelled 'High Coverage'. Strictly worse than the 'coverage from one source only' defect task-028 recorded, and undetected by the task audit.
resolution: ALREADY FIXED by task-028 — verified, not assumed. create_country_coverage_matrix was rewritten: the presence flag now comes from the joined-in side (an inner join against country_lookup projected to country_key), so a miss really reads 0, and the function's docstring records the old broken probe in past tense. The friction was captured during task-028 and describes a defect that task's own rewrite closed.

what: spec_v1 § Data Sources #2 (EPI) states 'Bronze table: bronze_epi2024results' / 'Silver table: silver_epi2024results' as fixed names, but bronze_ingest_epi now derives bronze_epi{p_epi_year}results and silver-to-gold2 derives silver_epi{EPI_YEAR}results, while bronze-to-silver.Notebook (lines 74/108) still hardcodes both. The year is parameterized at the two ends and hardcoded in the middle, and the spec documents only the 2024 instance. Reconciling means stating the naming convention plus the single-vintage caveat, and deciding whether bronze-to-silver should be parameterized too.

calculations.md documents the four unit-conversion factors but says nothing about units outside them, so the doc cannot distinguish the old silent-passthrough behavior from the new NULL quantity_base. gold_tables.md also needs the gold_dim_date UNKNOWN-DATE member (19000101), nullable quantity_base/spend_eur, and the new gold_unmapped_unit_audit table.
resolution: calculations.md now states that units outside the four-factor map yield NULL quantity_base AND NULL spend_eur rather than a passthrough, explains why NULL is the honest answer, names gold_unmapped_unit_audit, documents the lower(trim()) lookup, and cross-references the mirrored advisory DQ rule. Also flagged the EUR/kg basis as an UNVERIFIED assumption with the UNITPRICE_BASIS switch and the per_row_unit consequence (task-030 AC3). gold_tables.md: fact_procurement now shows date_key never NULL (19000101 sentinel), nullable quantity_base/spend_eur, and the previously undocumented source_row_id (verified present, and placed in the real select order); gold_dim_date documents the UNKNOWN-DATE member plus the Power-BI-date-table caveat; a new 'Audit & Observability Tables' section documents gold_unmapped_unit_audit in full (columns verified against the notebook) and inventories the other 12 gold tables that had no schema home at all.

what: task-031 changed the silver_wgi contract but two support docs still describe the retired CSV lineage: medallion_architecture.md line 142 says the WGI silver step is 'Unpivot year columns, filter to 2020, join with metadata' (that is the Excel/CSV pipeline, not the API long-format select), and data_quality_architecture.md line 237 annotates has_wgi as '(requires all 5 indicators)' when the rule now requires six. Also newly undocumented: silver_wgi's grain is now (country_iso3, indicator_name, year) with indicator_code/year/value columns, null-valued observations are dropped, and bronze-to-silver now HARD FAILS if bronze_WGI lacks Indicator Code/Year/Value. Delegated per task-031 AC4 ('cross-reference, don't double-do') — task-033 already owns WGI drift in both files.
resolution: medallion_architecture.md transformation 3 rewritten from the retired 'unpivot year columns, filter to 2020, join with metadata' to the live API lineage, with a note on why nothing is unpivoted; the table list now names silver_wgi with its real grain and columns instead of silver_WB. data_quality_architecture.md's gold_data_gaps tree: 'requires all 5 indicators' -> SIX, and — beyond what the friction recorded — every column name in that tree was wrong and was corrected against the notebook's select (country_name_std, country_role, has_epi_score, has_wgi_score, data_status with its real four values, spend_eur, transaction_count, calculated_at).

what: spec_v1 § Data Transformations -> 'Data Quality Rules Applied' still says 'Score range validation (0-100 for WB indicators)'. That is the retired dataflow's percentile-rank scale; silver_wgi now carries World Bank API ESTIMATES (approx -2.5..+2.5) and applies no range validation at all. The same section's item #3 was already corrected for Year/Value and six indicators, so this bullet is the residual half of that edit. Routes through /iterate.
source_anchor: spec_v1.md § Data Transformations -> Bronze to Silver -> 'Data Quality Rules Applied'

what: Two contradictory expectations of bronze_WGI's shape now coexist. bronze-to-silver HARD-REQUIRES ['Indicator Code','Year','Value']; data_quality_checks.Notebook asserts schema {'Country Name','Country Code','Series Name','Percentile Rank 2023'} with required fields ['Country Name','Country Code','Series Name']. Today bronze-to-silver fails first so the DQ activity never runs; after task-035 the DQ schema check will log an advisory fail (it is NOT in BLOCKING_CHECKS, so it cannot halt the pipeline). task-035 should absorb the DQ contract update.

.claude/commands/run-silver.md §3 'World Bank WGI Cleaning' documents the fully retired lineage: 'Input: bronze_WB_ESGCSV + bronze_WB_ESGSeries / Output: silver_WB / Unpivot year columns / Filter for year 2020 / Filter scores to 0-100 range'. None of those artifacts exist. FR-023's delegation to task-033 names only medallion_architecture.md:142 and data_quality_architecture.md:237, so this third location is uncovered.
resolution: run-silver.md swept of the retired WGI lineage in all five places it appeared, not just the one the friction named: the summary bullet (unpivoting), § 3 (rewritten to the bronze_WGI -> silver_wgi API lineage with an explicit note on why nothing is unpivoted and why there is no 0-100 range check), the validation table list (silver_WB -> silver_wgi), the expected row count (now the full time series), and the score-range check (replaced with a grain-uniqueness + six-indicator assertion). Added two troubleshooting entries for the RuntimeErrors task-031 introduced.
